name: Peach Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 1 pound peaches, stoned and cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the vinegar in a medium, nonreactive saucepan.
2. Warm over low heat just until it begins to give off vapor (do not bring to a boil).
3. Stir in the peach cubes and cook for 1 minute.
4. Pour the mixture into a sterilized 1-quart Mason jar.
5. Allow to cool to room temperature, 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Place a sterilized seal on top of the jar and screw on the ring.
7. Shake a few times to mix.
8. Store the jar out of direct sunlight and away from heat for 5 days, shaking it periodically while the mixture steeps (the vinegar will absorb most of the pigment from the fruit).
9. Strain the contents through a fine sieve into a 4-cup glass measuring cup.
10. Discard the fruit residue and rinse the sieve.
11. Rinse the Mason jar and return the strained vinegar to the jar.
12. Dampen a flat-bottom coffee filter and fit it into the sieve.
13. Rinse the measuring cup, place the sieve on top, and pour in the vinegar a bit at a time, allowing it to drip into the measuring cup.
14. Transfer the vinegar to flasks, bottles, or cruets.
15. The vinegar should be ready to use immediately with a shelf life of at least 1 year.
YIELD: 2 cups
VARIATION:
To make Peach Mint Vinegar, place 1 sprig fresh mint in the container before pouring in the vinegar. Fill, seal, and let steep out of direct sunlight and away from heat for 1 day before using or shipping.
